title: arctostaphylos glandulosa subsp. crassifolia
intro: Arctostaphylos glandulosa subsp. crassifolia, also known as the Bigberry Manzanita, is a shrub species endemic to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This drought-tolerant evergreen shrub is prized for its medicinal properties, particularly its high levels of antioxidants and phenolic compounds. The plant has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, with its medicinal properties attributed to its ability to stimulate digestion and reduce oxidative stress. Bigberry Manzanita is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, where it is valued for its adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties.
